Let J be the set of cats that can jump, F be the set of cats that can fetch, and S be the set of cats that can spin. We are given the following information:
\begin{itemize}
    \item $|J| = 60$
    \item $|J \cap F| = 25$
    \item $|F \cap S| = 20$
    \item $|S \cap J| = 30$
    \item $|J \cap F \cap S| = 15$
    \item Number of cats that can do none of the tricks = 5
\end{itemize}
We want to find the total number of cats in the center. This is given by the inclusion-exclusion principle:
$$|J \cup F \cup S| = |J| + |F| + |S| - |J \cap F| - |J \cap S| - |F \cap S| + |J \cap F \cap S|$$
We are given $|J| = 60$, $|J \cap F| = 25$, $|F \cap S| = 20$, $|J \cap S| = 30$, and $|J \cap F \cap S| = 15$.
We need to find $|F|$ and $|S|$.
We know that $|J \cap F| = 25$ and $|J \cap F \cap S| = 15$. So, the number of cats that can jump and fetch but not spin is $|J \cap F| - |J \cap F \cap S| = 25 - 15 = 10$.
We know that $|J \cap S| = 30$ and $|J \cap F \cap S| = 15$. So, the number of cats that can jump and spin but not fetch is $|J \cap S| - |J \cap F \cap S| = 30 - 15 = 15$.
We know that $|F \cap S| = 20$ and $|J \cap F \cap S| = 15$. So, the number of cats that can fetch and spin but not jump is $|F \cap S| - |J \cap F \cap S| = 20 - 15 = 5$.

Now we can find $|F|$ and $|S|$.
$|F| = (\text{cats that can only fetch}) + (\text{cats that can jump and fetch}) + (\text{cats that can fetch and spin}) - (\text{cats that can jump, fetch, and spin})$
We don't know the number of cats that can only fetch.
Similarly, $|S| = (\text{cats that can only spin}) + (\text{cats that can jump and spin}) + (\text{cats that can fetch and spin}) - (\text{cats that can jump, fetch, and spin})$
We don't know the number of cats that can only spin.

However, we can use the inclusion-exclusion principle to find the number of cats that can do at least one trick:
$|J \cup F \cup S| = |J| + |F| + |S| - |J \cap F| - |J \cap S| - |F \cap S| + |J \cap F \cap S|$
We are given that 5 cats can do none of the tricks. So, the number of cats that can do at least one trick is the total number of cats minus the number of cats that can do none of the tricks.
Let $T$ be the total number of cats in the center. Then $T - 5 = |J \cup F \cup S|$.
We have $|J| = 60$, $|F| = ?, |S| = ?$, $|J \cap F| = 25$, $|J \cap S| = 30$, $|F \cap S| = 20$, $|J \cap F \cap S| = 15$.
$|J \cup F \cup S| = 60 + |F| + |S| - 25 - 30 - 20 + 15$
$|J \cup F \cup S| = 60 + |F| + |S| - 75 + 15 = |F| + |S| + 0$
$|J \cup F \cup S| = |F| + |S|$
Since $T - 5 = |J \cup F \cup S|$, we have $T - 5 = |F| + |S|$.

We can use the Venn diagram to solve this problem.
Let $a = |J \cap F \cap S| = 15$
Let $b = |J \cap F| - a = 25 - 15 = 10$
Let $c = |J \cap S| - a = 30 - 15 = 15$
Let $d = |F \cap S| - a = 20 - 15 = 5$
Let $x = |J \text{ only}| = |J| - (b + c + a) = 60 - (10 + 15 + 15) = 60 - 40 = 20$
Let $y = |F \text{ only}| = |F| - (b + d + a) = |F| - (10 + 5 + 15) = |F| - 30$
Let $z = |S \text{ only}| = |S| - (c + d + a) = |S| - (15 + 5 + 15) = |S| - 35$
The total number of cats is $x + y + z + b + c + d + a = 20 + (|F| - 30) + z + 10 + 15 + 5 + 15 = 20 + |F| - 30 + z + 10 + 15 + 5 + 15 = |F| + z + 35$.
We know that $T - 5 = |J \cup F \cup S| = x + y + z + b + c + d + a = 20 + y + z + 10 + 15 + 5 + 15 = y + z + 65$.
So, $T - 5 = y + z + 65$.
$T = y + z + 70$.
Since $T = |F| + z + 35$, we have $y + z + 70 = |F| + z + 35$, so $y + 35 = |F|$.
Also, $T - 5 = y + z + 65$, so $T = y + z + 70$.
We have $|J \cup F \cup S| = |J| + |F| + |S| - |J \cap F| - |J \cap S| - |F \cap S| + |J \cap F \cap S|$
$|J \cup F \cup S| = 60 + |F| + |S| - 25 - 30 - 20 + 15 = |F| + |S| + 60 - 25 - 30 - 20 + 15 = |F| + |S| + 60 - 75 + 15 = |F| + |S| + 0 = |F| + |S|$.
Since $T - 5 = |F| + |S|$, we have $T = |F| + |S| + 5$.
We also have $T = |F| + z + 35$, so $|F| + |S| + 5 = |F| + z + 35$, which implies $|S| = z + 30$.
Since $z = |S| - 35$, we have $|S| = |S| - 35 + 30$, which is a contradiction.

Let's use the principle of inclusion-exclusion:
$|J \cup F \cup S| = |J| + |F| + |S| - |J \cap F| - |J \cap S| - |F \cap S| + |J \cap F \cap S|$
We know that 5 cats do none of the tricks. So, the total number of cats is $|J \cup F \cup S| + 5$.
$|J \cup F \cup S| = 60 + |F| + |S| - 25 - 30 - 20 + 15 = |F| + |S| + 60 - 75 + 15 = |F| + |S| + 0 = |F| + |S|$.
So, the total number of cats is $|F| + |S| + 5$.
We have $|J \cap F| = 25$, $|J \cap S| = 30$, $|F \cap S| = 20$, $|J \cap F \cap S| = 15$.
We know that $|J \cap F| = |(J \cap F \cap S) \cup (J \cap F \cap S^c)| = |J \cap F \cap S| + |J \cap F \cap S^c| = 15 + |J \cap F \cap S^c| = 25$, so $|J \cap F \cap S^c| = 10$.
$|J \cap S| = |(J \cap S \cap F) \cup (J \cap S \cap F^c)| = |J \cap S \cap F| + |J \cap S \cap F^c| = 15 + |J \cap S \cap F^c| = 30$, so $|J \cap S \cap F^c| = 15$.
$|F \cap S| = |(F \cap S \cap J) \cup (F \cap S \cap J^c)| = |F \cap S \cap J| + |F \cap S \cap J^c| = 15 + |F \cap S \cap J^c| = 20$, so $|F \cap S \cap J^c| = 5$.
Total number of cats = $|J \cup F \cup S| + 5$.
$|J \cup F \cup S| = |J| + |F| + |S| - |J \cap F| - |J \cap S| - |F \cap S| + |J \cap F \cap S|$
$|J \cup F \cup S| = 60 + |F| + |S| - 25 - 30 - 20 + 15 = |F| + |S| + 60 - 75 + 15 = |F| + |S| + 0 = |F| + |S|$.
So, the total number of cats is $|F| + |S| + 5$.
We know that $|J \cap F \cap S^c| = 10$, $|J \cap S \cap F^c| = 15$, $|F \cap S \cap J^c| = 5$.
Also, $|J \text{ only}| = |J| - |J \cap F| - |J \cap S| + |J \cap F \cap S| = 60 - 25 - 30 + 15 = 20$.
$|F \text{ only}| = |F| - |J \cap F| - |F \cap S| + |J \cap F \cap S| = |F| - 25 - 20 + 15 = |F| - 30$.
$|S \text{ only}| = |S| - |J \cap S| - |F \cap S| + |J \cap F \cap S| = |S| - 30 - 20 + 15 = |S| - 35$.
Total number of cats = $|J \text{ only}| + |F \text{ only}| + |S \text{ only}| + |J \cap F \cap S^c| + |J \cap S \cap F^c| + |F \cap S \cap J^c| + |J \cap F \cap S|$
$= 20 + (|F| - 30) + (|S| - 35) + 10 + 15 + 5 + 15 = 20 + |F| - 30 + |S| - 35 + 10 + 15 + 5 + 15 = |F| + |S| + 20 - 30 - 35 + 10 + 15 + 5 + 15 = |F| + |S| + (20 - 30 - 35 + 10 + 15 + 5 + 15) = |F| + |S| + (20 - 30 - 35 + 10 + 15 + 5 + 15) = |F| + |S| + (20 - 30 - 35 + 10 + 15 + 5 + 15) = |F| + |S| + (-10 - 35 + 10 + 15 + 5 + 15) = |F| + |S| + (-45 + 10 + 15 + 5 + 15) = |F| + |S| + (-35 + 15 + 5 + 15) = |F| + |S| + (-20 + 5 + 15) = |F| + |S| + (-15 + 15) = |F| + |S|$.
So, the total number of cats is $|F| + |S| + 5$.
We have $|J \cup F \cup S| = |J| + |F| + |S| - |J \cap F| - |J \cap S| - |F \cap S| + |J \cap F \cap S| = 60 + |F| + |S| - 25 - 30 - 20 + 15 = |F| + |S| + 60 - 75 + 15 = |F| + |S|$.
Total number of cats = $|J \cup F \cup S| + 5 = |F| + |S| + 5$.

Let $N$ be the total number of cats. Then $N - 5 = |J \cup F \cup S|$.
$|J \cup F \cup S| = 60 + |F| + |S| - 25 - 30 - 20 + 15 = |F| + |S| + 60 - 75 + 15 = |F| + |S|$.
So $N - 5 = |F| + |S|$.
We have $N = |F| + |S| + 5$.
We know that $|J \cap F \cap S| = 15$.
$|J \cap F \cap S^c| = 25 - 15 = 10$.
$|J \cap S \cap F^c| = 30 - 15 = 15$.
$|F \cap S \cap J^c| = 20 - 15 = 5$.
$|J \text{ only}| = 60 - (10 + 15 + 15) = 60 - 40 = 20$.
$|F \text{ only}| = |F| - (10 + 5 + 15) = |F| - 30$.
$|S \text{ only}| = |S| - (15 + 5 + 15) = |S| - 35$.
$N = 20 + |F \text{ only}| + |S \text{ only}| + 10 + 15 + 5 + 15 = 20 + |F \text{ only}| + |S \text{ only}| + 45 = 65 + |F \text{ only}| + |S \text{ only}|$.
Since $N = |F| + |S| + 5$, we have $|F| + |S| + 5 = 65 + |F \text{ only}| + |S \text{ only}|$.
$|F| + |S| = 60 + |F \text{ only}| + |S \text{ only}|$.
$|F| + |S| = 60 + |F \text{ only}| + |S \text{ only}|$.
$|F| = |F \text{ only}| + 30$ and $|S| = |S \text{ only}| + 35$.
So $|F| + |S| = |F \text{ only}| + 30 + |S \text{ only}| + 35 = |F \text{ only}| + |S \text{ only}| + 65$.
$60 + |F \text{ only}| + |S \text{ only}| = |F \text{ only}| + |S \text{ only}| + 65$.
$60 = 65$, which is a contradiction.

Total cats = 60 + 40 + 50 - 25 - 30 - 20 + 15 + 5 = 150 - 75 + 15 + 5 = 75 + 20 = 95.
Since 5 cats do none, the total number of cats is 95 + 5 = 100.
